name: Ceremony
description: Dual analog 18dB/oct resonant twin-peak filter inspired by Rob Hordijk's TwinPeak. Morphs continuously from lowpass to twin-peak bandpass, crossfades two inputs (voltage-controlled), self-oscillates and pings cleanly, and adds cross-filter FM, per-input feedback and an output VCA.

controls.name: Freq A
controls.description: Sets the first resonant peak frequency.

controls.name: Freq B
controls.description: Sets the second resonant peak frequency.

controls.name: Freq A CV atten
controls.description: Attenuverter for the Freq A CV input.

controls.name: Freq B CV atten
controls.description: Attenuverter for the Freq B CV input.

controls.name: Both
controls.description: Moves both peaks together while preserving the interval set by Freq A and Freq B.

controls.name: LP/BP
controls.description: Morphs the response continuously from 18dB lowpass toward twin-peak bandpass.

controls.name: Resonance
controls.description: Raises Q on both peaks until the filter self-oscillates and pings cleanly.
controls.detail: voltage controlled · pings audio inputs at high settings

controls.name: Feed A
controls.description: Routes Audio In A back into Freq A control for richer harmonic content.

controls.name: Feed B
controls.description: Routes Audio In B back into Freq B control for richer harmonic content.

controls.name: FM Mod
controls.description: Sets how much Filter B's output modulates Frequency A.

controls.name: XFade
controls.description: Blends Audio In A and Audio In B into the filter core. Acts as a VCA on either input when the other is unpatched.

inputs.name: Audio In A
inputs.description: First audio input feeding the voltage-controlled crossfader.

inputs.name: Audio In B
inputs.description: Second audio input feeding the voltage-controlled crossfader.

inputs.name: XFade CV
inputs.description: Voltage controls the input crossfade between A and B.

inputs.name: Freq A CV
inputs.description: Attenuverted frequency CV for filter A.

inputs.name: Freq B CV
inputs.description: Attenuverted frequency CV for filter B.

inputs.name: Both CV
inputs.description: Tracks 1V/oct and shifts both peaks together while preserving the interval between Freq A and Freq B.

inputs.name: FM CV
inputs.description: Controls the amount of cross-filter FM (Filter B output → Frequency A).

inputs.name: VCA CV
inputs.description: Controls the output VCA on the main output.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Main filtered output, level-controlled by the output VCA.

behaviors.name: Continuous LP → twin-peak BP morph
behaviors.description: Response morphs continuously from 18dB lowpass to a twin-peak bandpass shape with two independently tunable resonant peaks — no mode switching.

behaviors.name: Interval-preserving frequency link
behaviors.description: Both CV transposes the pair of peaks together while keeping the interval set by Freq A vs Freq B intact, so chord-like dual resonances track pitch.

behaviors.name: Cross-filter FM
behaviors.description: Filter B's output is fed into Frequency A as FM, producing sideband colour that scales with resonance and FM depth.

behaviors.name: Per-input feedback coloration
behaviors.description: Routing each audio input back into its own frequency control adds harmonics, vocal formants and screaming/ringing colours; pairs especially well with simple waves.

behaviors.name: Self-oscillation & pinging
behaviors.description: At high resonance the two peaks self-oscillate as sine voices; trigs/clicks fed into Audio In A/B ping them for plucky, bell-like, dual-pitch percussion.

behaviors.name: VC input crossfader / pre-VCA
behaviors.description: Crossfader between Audio In A/B is voltage-controlled; with one input unpatched it doubles as a pre-filter VCA on the other for tremolo or duck-into-resonance moves.

behaviors.name: Output dynamics VCA
behaviors.description: Main output passes through a dedicated VCA so envelopes/LFOs shape level without an external VCA — useful for plucks, gating self-oscillation, etc.

patchIdeas.name: Twin-peak bell (1V/oct pinged voice)
patchIdeas.patch: Crank resonance into self-oscillation, ping with gates on Audio In A, and play pitch into Both CV. The two peaks track together as a dual-partial bell tuned by the Freq A/B interval.
steps: Set Resonance to ~3 o'clock so both peaks just self-oscillate (you should hear a quiet sine pair).
Set LP/BP to noon so both peaks are clearly audible.
Set Freq A to noon and Freq B about a fifth above to hand-tune the bell interval.
Set FM Mod fully CCW (off) and Feed A/B fully CCW for a clean pinged tone.
Patch Sequencer (Pitch) >> Ceremony (Both CV) so 1V/oct transposes both peaks together.
Patch Sequencer (Gate) >> Ceremony (Audio In A) to ping the resonators on each note.
Patch Envelope >> Ceremony (VCA CV) so each ping has a short decay shape.
Patch Ceremony (OUT) >> Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Ceremony OUT — plucky two-partial bell, pitched by Both CV.
patchIdeas.hear: A clean dual-tone bell that transposes in tune; widening the Freq A/B interval changes it from chime to gamelan-like clang. Add Feed A a hair for a darker, woodier strike.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ceremony:
* Resonance: ~3 o'clock (just self-oscillating)
* LP/BP: noon
* Freq A: noon
* Freq B: noon + ~fifth
* FM Mod: off
* Feed A/B: off

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ceremony (Both CV)
- Sequencer (Gate) g> Ceremony (Audio In A)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ceremony (VCA CV)
- Ceremony (OUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Crossfaded dual-source resonant filter
patchIdeas.patch: Run two oscillators into A and B, sweep the input crossfader with an LFO, and use the LP/BP morph as a tone control. The output glues both sources into one moving resonant image.
steps: Set LP/BP to about 10 o'clock (mostly lowpass, a hint of peaks).
Set Resonance to ~1 o'clock for a sung/vocal colour without self-oscillation.
Set Feed A/B and FM Mod to off for a clean filter sound.
Set Freq A around noon and Freq B a third above to colour the bandpass shoulder.
Patch VCO A (Saw) -> Ceremony (Audio In A).
Patch VCO B (Saw, detuned) -> Ceremony (Audio In B).
Patch slow LFO >> Ceremony (XFade CV) so the mix slowly morphs A↔B.
Patch keyboard / sequencer (Pitch) >> Ceremony (Both CV) so the filter tracks the played note.
Patch Ceremony (OUT) >> VCA / Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Ceremony OUT — single filtered voice that morphs between the two oscillator timbres.
patchIdeas.hear: A vocal, slowly breathing dual-saw pad that drifts between source A and source B; raise resonance and Freq B for a more nasal/formant character.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ceremony:
* LP/BP: ~10 o'clock
* Resonance: ~1 o'clock
* Freq A: noon
* Freq B: noon + third
* Feed A/B: off
* FM Mod: off

- VCO A (Saw) -> Ceremony (Audio In A)
- VCO B (Saw) -> Ceremony (Audio In B) // detuned
- Slow LFO (Out) >> Ceremony (XFade CV)
- Keys (Pitch) p> Ceremony (Both CV)
- Ceremony (OUT) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-oscillating twin-peak drone
patchIdeas.patch: No external audio — just self-oscillation. Use a slow LFO on Both CV to drift the dyad and FM CV for slow inharmonic shimmer. The module becomes a pitched twin-peak drone voice.
steps: Leave Audio In A and Audio In B unpatched.
Set LP/BP to ~3 o'clock so both peaks are loud.
Set Resonance to max so both peaks self-oscillate cleanly.
Set Freq A and Freq B to taste — a wide interval (e.g. octave + fifth) gives a chord-like drone.
Set FM Mod to ~10 o'clock for a touch of cross-FM shimmer.
Patch slow LFO >> Ceremony (Both CV) to drift both peaks in unison.
Patch second slow LFO >> Ceremony (FM CV) to wobble the inharmonic content.
Open VCA CV with a fixed offset (or LFO) so the OUT stays audible.
Patch Ceremony (OUT) >> Reverb -> Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Ceremony OUT — pitched dual-sine drone with slow harmonic motion.
patchIdeas.hear: A glassy, slowly tilting two-note drone; the two peaks beat against each other and the FM LFO adds shimmering sidebands. Push Resonance back a hair for breathier drift.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ceremony:
* LP/BP: ~3 o'clock
* Resonance: max
* Freq A: low
* Freq B: high (wide interval)
* FM Mod: ~10 o'clock
* Feed A/B: off

- Slow LFO 1 (Out) >> Ceremony (Both CV)
- Slow LFO 2 (Out) >> Ceremony (FM CV)
- Offset (Out) >> Ceremony (VCA CV)
- Ceremony (OUT) -> Reverb (In)
- Reverb (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Feedback acid line
patchIdeas.patch: Feed a saw into A, raise Feed A and FM Mod, and sweep Freq A with an envelope. The audio-into-frequency feedback turns it from clean to vocal to gritty as you push it.
steps: Set LP/BP to ~2 o'clock so the bandpass peaks dominate.
Set Freq A to ~10 o'clock and Freq B to ~1 o'clock so they spread.
Set Resonance to ~2 o'clock — hot but not self-oscillating yet.
Set Feed A to ~1 o'clock so the input bites back into the cutoff.
Set FM Mod to ~11 o'clock for cross-filter grit.
Patch VCO (Saw) -> Ceremony (Audio In A).
Patch Sequencer (Pitch) >> Ceremony (Both CV) for melodic 1V/oct tracking.
Patch Envelope >> Ceremony (Freq A CV) (set Freq A CV attenuverter ~2 o'clock) for the classic acid wow.
Patch Envelope >> Ceremony (VCA CV) so each note is shaped at the output.
Patch Ceremony (OUT) -> Output.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Ceremony OUT — squelchy acid voice with biting envelope sweep.
patchIdeas.hear: A vocal, growling lead that overshoots into screaming territory at the top of each envelope; pull Feed A back for a cleaner 303 tone, push FM Mod CW for gnarly inharmonic spit.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Ceremony:
* LP/BP: ~2 o'clock
* Freq A: ~10 o'clock
* Freq B: ~1 o'clock
* Resonance: ~2 o'clock
* Feed A: ~1 o'clock
* FM Mod: ~11 o'clock
* Freq A CV atten: ~2 o'clock

- VCO (Saw) -> Ceremony (Audio In A)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Ceremony (Both CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ceremony (Freq A CV)
- Envelope (Out) >> Ceremony (VCA CV)
- Ceremony (OUT) -> Output (In)
